refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall provide worksite area analysis including terrain representation.
refined_fr.rationale: The original FR includes multiple obligations: analysis of terrain, obstacles, and work-zone representation. To ensure atomicity and solution neutrality, these should be split into separate FRs, each focusing on a single aspect of worksite area analysis. This refinement isolates terrain representation as one atomic FR.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Verify that the system can generate and provide a representation of the terrain within the worksite area, independent of obstacle or work-zone data.
sub_frs: The NGHE solution shall provide worksite area analysis including terrain representation.
The NGHE solution shall provide worksite area analysis including obstacle representation.
The NGHE solution shall provide worksite area analysis including work-zone representation.

candidate_dps: Generate a digital terrain model representing the worksite topography
Create a semantic map encoding key site features relevant to planning and safety
Produce a unified worksite model integrating terrain and feature data for use by downstream functions
Provide separate data layers for terrain and site features accessible via a common interface
selected_dp.selected_dp: Produce a unified worksite model integrating terrain and feature data for use by downstream functions

potential_sources: Integration of terrain and feature data into a single model may create dependencies between data types
coupling_check.notes: While integrating terrain and site features into a unified model introduces some coupling, it supports consistency and ease of access for planning and execution functions, which is critical for safety and operational policies.

assumptions_risks: Assumes availability of accurate terrain and site feature data sources
Risk that integration complexity may delay model availability
Assumes planning and execution functions can consume unified model format
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Clarify the scope of site features to be included and specify interface requirements for the unified model to ensure modularity and extensibility.

im_boundary.name: NGHE Solution
im_boundary.scope: The NGHE Solution module encompasses the core data processing and integration functions, including the production of a unified worksite model by integrating terrain and feature data for downstream use.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Receives raw terrain and feature data from sensors and vehicle systems.

external_interfaces.name: Tasking
external_interfaces.details: Sends processed unified worksite model data to downstream functions and modules.

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: Facilitates data exchange with external systems and remote modules for model updates and synchronization.

module_containment_scores.notes: High cohesion as the module is designed for data integration and model production; minimal diffusion since most processing is centralized here.
Vehicle module handles raw data collection but not integration; would require significant diffusion to other modules for model production.
Primarily focused on remote control and monitoring; poor fit for data integration tasks.
Focused on safety alerts and monitoring; integrating worksite model is peripheral and would cause diffusion.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: The NGHE Solution module is the most appropriate boundary for containing the DP because it is specifically responsible for data processing and integration tasks, ensuring high cohesion and minimal diffusion of the unified worksite model functionality.
containment_risks: Potential dependency on timely and accurate data from Vehicle module may cause coupling issues.
If downstream functions span multiple modules, interface management complexity may increase.
Changes in data formats or sources could require frequent updates to NGHE Solution interfaces.

fr_dp_gate.reason: The DP directly addresses the FR by specifying the production of a unified worksite model integrating terrain and feature data, which fulfills the requirement to provide worksite area analysis including terrain representation.

diffusion_risks: Potential dependency on timely and accurate data from Vehicle module may cause coupling issues.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: The NGHE Solution module is well-contained with high cohesion and minimal diffusion risk, as it centralizes data integration and model production. Other modules have low containment scores and high diffusion scores, indicating poor fit for this DP.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Verification is feasible by confirming that the NGHE Solution module produces a worksite model/map available to planning and execution functions as per acceptance criteria.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: The ownership of the DP by the NGHE Solution module is clear and appropriate, with well-defined interfaces for data input and output.

recommended_revisions: Monitor and manage dependencies on the Vehicle module to mitigate coupling risks.
Establish robust interface versioning and update protocols to handle changes in data formats or sources.
Consider interface complexity management if downstream functions span multiple modules to reduce integration overhead.

decision.justification: All gates (FR->DP, DP->IM, FR->IM) have passed successfully, indicating that the current level of decomposition is sufficient. The DP directly addresses the FR, the DP is well-contained within the NGHE Solution module with manageable diffusion risks, and the ownership/allocation is clear and verifiable. Given the high safety criticality, it is prudent to avoid unnecessary further decomposition that could introduce complexity and integration overhead. Instead, focus should be on managing identified risks and ensuring robust interfaces.

revise.why: To address the identified coupling risks and interface complexity, revisions should focus on the implementation module level by monitoring dependencies on the Vehicle module and establishing robust interface versioning and update protocols.

assumptions: The NGHE Solution module can effectively centralize data integration and model production.
Dependencies on the Vehicle module can be managed through interface controls.
open_risks: Potential dependency on timely and accurate data from Vehicle module causing coupling issues.
Interface complexity if downstream functions span multiple modules.
evidence_needed: Verification that the NGHE Solution module produces the unified worksite model as per acceptance criteria.
Demonstration of effective interface versioning and update protocols.
Monitoring reports on dependency management with the Vehicle module.
